003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.toml;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
027
028/**
029 * Serializes POJOs to TOML v1.0.0 format.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * TOML (Tom's Obvious Minimal Language) maps unambiguously to hash tables with native typing.
033 * See <a href="https://toml.io/en/v1.0.0">TOML v1.0.0</a> specification.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='topic'>Media types</h5>
036 * <p>
037 * Produces: <bc>application/toml</bc>
038 * <br>Accepts: <bc>application/toml</bc>
039 *
040 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean-to-TOML mapping</h5>
041 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
042 *    <li>Simple properties → key-value pairs
043 *    <li>Nested beans → <c>[tableName]</c> sections
044 *    <li>Collections of beans → <c>[[tableName]]</c> array of tables
045 *    <li>Collections of simple values → <c>key = [a, b, c]</c>
046 *    <li>Maps with string keys → <c>[mapName]</c> table
047 *    <li>Null properties are omitted (TOML has no null type)
048 * </ul>
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
051 * <p class='bjava'>
052 *    <jc>// Serialize a bean to TOML</jc>
053 *    String <jv>toml</jv> = TomlSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.serialize(<jv>myBean</jv>);
054 *
055 *    <jc>// Serialize a map (e.g. config with nested sections)</jc>
056 *    Map&lt;String, Object&gt; <jv>config</jv> = Map.of(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"myapp"</js>, <js>"port"</js>, 8080);
057 *    <jv>toml</jv> = TomlSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.serialize(<jv>config</jv>);
058 *
059 *    <jc>// Use the marshaller for convenience</jc>
060 *    <jv>toml</jv> = Toml.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>config</jv>);
061 *
062 *    <jc>// Custom serializer with whitespace for readability</jc>
063 *    TomlSerializer <jv>s</jv> = TomlSerializer.<jsm>create</jsm>().ws().build();
064 *    <jv>toml</jv> = <jv>s</jv>.serialize(<jv>config</jv>);
065 * </p>
066 *
067 * <h5 class='figure'>Example output (Map of name/age):</h5>
068 * <p class='bcode'>
069 *    name = "Alice"
070 *    age = 30
071 * </p>
072 *
073 * <h5 class='figure'>Complex (nested table + array):</h5>
074 * <p class='bcode'>
075 *    name = "Alice"
076 *    age = 30
077 *    tags = ["a", "b", "c"]
078 *
079 *    [address]
080 *    street = "123 Main St"
081 *    city = "Boston"
082 *    state = "MA"
083 * </p>
084 *
085 * <h5 class='topic'>Limitations compared to JSON</h5>
086 * <p>
087 * TOML is a configuration format with a more restricted type system than JSON.
088 * The following features supported by {@link JsonSerializer}
089 * are not available or limited in TOML:
090 * </p>
091 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
092 *    <li><b>Null values</b> — TOML has no null type. By default, null properties are omitted.
093 *       Use <c>keepNullProperties()</c> to write the configured <c>nullValue</c> string (e.g. <c>&lt;NULL&gt;</c>) instead.
094 *    <li><b>Map keys</b> — TOML tables only support string keys. Maps with non-string keys
095 *       (e.g. <c>Map&lt;Integer,String&gt;</c>) have keys converted via <c>toString()</c>; ensure results are valid TOML keys.
096 *       Null keys are serialized as the string <c>null</c>.
097 *    <li><b>Polymorphic types</b> — <c>addBeanTypes()</c> and <c>addRootType()</c> add <c>_type</c>
098 *       discriminators for polymorphic parsing; use with care as TOML structure differs from JSON.
099 *    <li><b>Root-level collections</b> — TOML expects a root table. Root arrays use array-of-tables
100 *       (<c>[[item]]</c>) for beans/maps, or a <c>_value</c> wrapper for primitives and simple arrays.
101 *    <li><b>Duplicate keys</b> — TOML forbids duplicate keys; structures that would produce duplicate
102 *       keys may yield invalid TOML.
103 * </ul>
104 *
105 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
106 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
107 * </ul>
108 */
109@SuppressWarnings({
110   "java:S110", "java:S115",
111   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's serializer session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
112})
113public class TomlSerializer extends WriterSerializer implements RecordWritable {
114
115   private static final String PROP_inlineTableThreshold = "inlineTableThreshold";
116   private static final String PROP_useInlineTables = "useInlineTables";
117   private static final String PROP_sortKeys = "sortKeys";
118   private static final String PROP_nullValue = "nullValue";
119   private static final String ARG_copyFrom = "copyFrom";
120
121   /**
122    * Builder class.
123    */
124   public static class Builder extends WriterSerializer.Builder<Builder> {
125
126      private static final Cache<HashKey,TomlSerializer> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, TomlSerializer.class).build();
127
128      private int inlineTableThreshold = 3;
129      private boolean useInlineTables = true;
130      private boolean sortKeys = false;
131      private String nullValue = "<NULL>";
132
133      protected Builder() {
134         produces("application/toml");
135         accept("application/toml");
136      }
137
138      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
139         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
140         inlineTableThreshold = copyFrom.inlineTableThreshold;
141         useInlineTables = copyFrom.useInlineTables;
142         sortKeys = copyFrom.sortKeys;
143         nullValue = copyFrom.nullValue;
144      }
145
146      protected Builder(TomlSerializer copyFrom) {
147         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
148         inlineTableThreshold = copyFrom.inlineTableThreshold;
149         useInlineTables = copyFrom.useInlineTables;
150         sortKeys = copyFrom.sortKeys;
151         nullValue = copyFrom.nullValue;
152      }
153
154      /**
155       * String to write for null values. Parser treats values matching this as null.
156       *
157       * <p>
158       * Default is {@code <NULL>} to avoid confusion with the literal string {@code "null"}.
159       *
160       * @param value The null marker string.
161       * @return This object.
162       */
163      public Builder nullValue(String value) {
164         nullValue = value;
165         return this;
166      }
167
168      /**
169       * Maximum properties for inline table format.
170       *
171       * @param value The threshold.
172       * @return This object.
173       */
174      public Builder inlineTableThreshold(int value) {
175         inlineTableThreshold = value;
176         return this;
177      }
178
179      /**
180       * Use inline tables when possible.
181       *
182       * @param value The flag.
183       * @return This object.
184       */
185      public Builder useInlineTables(boolean value) {
186         useInlineTables = value;
187         return this;
188      }
189
190      /**
191       * Sort keys alphabetically in output.
192       *
193       * @param value The flag.
194       * @return This object.
195       */
196      public Builder sortKeys(boolean value) {
197         sortKeys = value;
198         return this;
199      }
200
201      @Override
202      public Builder useWhitespace() {
203         super.useWhitespace();
204         return this;
205      }
206
207      @Override
208      public Builder useWhitespace(boolean value) {
209         super.useWhitespace(value);
210         return this;
211      }
212
213      @Override
214      public Builder ws() {
215         return useWhitespace();
216      }
217
218      @Override
219      public TomlSerializer build() {
220         return cache(CACHE).build(TomlSerializer.class);
221      }
222
223      @Override
224      public Builder copy() {
225         return new Builder(this);
226      }
227
228      @Override
229      public HashKey hashKey() {
230         return HashKey.of(super.hashKey(), inlineTableThreshold, useInlineTables, sortKeys, nullValue);
231      }
232   }
233
234   /** Default serializer. */
235   public static final TomlSerializer DEFAULT = new TomlSerializer(create());
236
237   /** Default serializer with blank lines between sections. */
238   public static final TomlSerializer DEFAULT_READABLE = new TomlSerializer(create().ws());
239
240   public static Builder create() {
241      return new Builder();
242   }
243
244   protected final int inlineTableThreshold;
245   protected final boolean useInlineTables;
246   protected final boolean sortKeys;
247   /** String written for null values. */
248   protected final String nullValue;
249
250   /**
251    * Constructor.
252    *
253    * @param builder The builder.
254    */
255   public TomlSerializer(Builder builder) {
256      super(builder);
257      inlineTableThreshold = builder.inlineTableThreshold;
258      useInlineTables = builder.useInlineTables;
259      sortKeys = builder.sortKeys;
260      nullValue = builder.nullValue != null ? builder.nullValue : "<NULL>";
261   }
262
263   /**
264    * Returns the string written for null values.
265    *
266    * @return The null marker.
267    */
268   public String getNullValue() {
269      return nullValue;
270   }
271
272   @Override
273   public TomlSerializerSession.Builder createSession() {
274      return TomlSerializerSession.create(this);
275   }
276
277   @Override
278   public Builder copy() {
279      return new Builder(this);
280   }
281
282   @Override
283   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
284      return super.properties()
285         .a(PROP_inlineTableThreshold, inlineTableThreshold)
286         .a(PROP_useInlineTables, useInlineTables)
287         .a(PROP_sortKeys, sortKeys)
288         .a(PROP_nullValue, nullValue);
289   }
290
291   /**
292    * Convenience delegator that opens a {@link RecordWriter} over the output using
293    * <b>default session arguments</b> (mirrors {@link #serialize(Object)}).
294    *
295    * <p>
296    * The real implementation lives on {@link TomlSerializerSession#serializeRecords(Object)}.
297    * Callers that need request-derived configuration should call {@link #createSession()} and
298    * invoke {@link TomlSerializerSession#serializeRecords(Object)} on the built session instead.
299    *
300    * @param output The output.
301    * @return A new {@link RecordWriter}.
302    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
303    */
304   @Override /* RecordWritable */
305   public RecordWriter serializeRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
306      return ((RecordWritable) getSession()).serializeRecords(output);
307   }
308
309   @Override /* RecordWritable */
310   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
311      return false;
312   }
313}
